summary: Iteration 36 - Vulnerability Assessment feature testing complete. Backend: 24/24 tests passed (100%). Frontend: VA page loads correctly with all components. The VA feature correctly analyzes 13 devices, detects 22 vulnerabilities (8 critical, 2 high, 12 medium), calculates security score of 81/100, and provides 5 prioritized remediation actions.

minor.issue: Returns 500 error (unrelated to VA feature - existing issue with AlertResponse validation)

integration_issues.issue: Token sometimes lost when navigating between pages, causing 401 errors. User gets redirected to login. This is an existing auth issue, not specific to VA.

backend_api: GET /api/vulnerability/dashboard/{client_id} - Returns 200 with all required fields
Dashboard returns overall_score: 81/100
Dashboard returns risk_level: 'medio'
Dashboard returns total_devices: 13
Dashboard returns total_vulnerabilities: 22
Dashboard returns severity_counts: {critical: 8, high: 2, medium: 12, low: 0}
Dashboard returns devices array with device_ip, device_name, score, vuln_count, reachable
Dashboard returns vulnerabilities array sorted by severity (critical first)
Dashboard returns remediation_summary with 5 prioritized actions
GET /api/vulnerability/device/{client_id}/{device_ip} - Returns 200 with device details
Device detail returns vulnerabilities, open_ports, monitor_type, community
Device detail returns 404 for invalid IP
POST /api/vulnerability/run-scan/{client_id} - Returns 200 with scan results
Run scan returns id, timestamp, overall_score, total_devices, total_vulnerabilities
Run scan returns 404 for invalid client
GET /api/vulnerability/history/{client_id} - Returns 200 with scan history array
History entries have required fields (id, client_id, timestamp, overall_score)
Detects risky open ports (Telnet, FTP, RDP, SMB)
Detects default SNMP community strings ('public')
Detects HTTP interfaces without HTTPS
Detects weak protocols (SNMPv1/v2c)
Dashboard requires authentication (401 without token)
Run scan requires authentication (401 without token)
Device detail requires authentication (401 without token)
frontend_ui: VA page accessible from SICUREZZA menu in sidebar
Score gauge displays 81/100 with circular progress
Risk level label shows 'RISCHIO MEDIO' with appropriate color
Stats cards show: 13 devices, 22 vulnerabilities, 8 critical, 2 high
Severity distribution bar with colors (red=critical, orange=high, yellow=medium, blue=low)
Tab 'Dispositivi' shows device list with individual scores
Tab 'Piano di Remediation' shows prioritized actions
Device cards show score gauge, name, IP, reachable status, vuln counts
Client select dropdown visible for changing analyzed client

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix session persistence issue where token is lost on navigation (existing auth issue, not VA-specific)
OPTIONAL: Fix /api/alerts 500 error (AlertResponse validation issue, not VA-related)
critical_code_review_comments: vulnerability.py: Well-structured knowledge base for risky ports with severity levels and remediation guidance
vulnerability.py: Correct scoring algorithm - deductions based on severity (critical=25, high=15, medium=8, low=3)
vulnerability.py: Proper detection of default SNMP communities (public, private, community, etc.)
vulnerability.py: HTTP without HTTPS detection working correctly
vulnerability.py: SNMPv1/v2c weak protocol detection implemented
vulnerability.py: Remediation summary correctly groups actions by priority
VulnerabilityPage.js: Clean component structure with ScoreGauge, StatCard, DevicesTab, VulnsTab, RemediationTab
VulnerabilityPage.js: Proper data-testid attributes for all interactive elements
VulnerabilityPage.js: Device expansion with detailed vulnerability view working

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all UI components verified, minor auth session issue)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Using existing demo data: 13 devices with open ports, SNMP community 'public', devices with HTTP without HTTPS.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 36 verified the Vulnerability Assessment feature. All VA endpoints working: dashboard (score 81/100, 22 vulns), device detail, run-scan, history. Frontend VA page loads correctly with score gauge, severity distribution, 3 tabs (Dispositivi, Vulnerabilità, Piano di Remediation), device expansion, and run scan button. Minor issue: session token sometimes lost on navigation (existing auth issue). The /api/alerts endpoint has a 500 error (AlertResponse validation) but this is unrelated to VA.

mocked_apis.firebase_push: Firebase Cloud Messaging (push notifications) is MOCKED

scan_history.returns: Array of past scans with timestamps and scores

frontend_components.score_gauge: Circular gauge with score and risk label
frontend_components.device_expansion: Click to expand device details with vulnerabilities
